task_completion.description: Verify that Screaming Frog SEO Spider was used to crawl the target website.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Screaming Frog SEO Spider application window should be visible with 'https://crawler-test.com/' entered in the URL bar at the top, and the main window should display crawl results or progress.

task_completion.description: Verify the Hreflang data was exported as a CSV file to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or Screaming Frog save dialog showing a CSV file with 'hreflang' in its filename located in the '~/Documents/SEO/exports/' (or '/home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/') directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the Internal HTML report was exported as a CSV file to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or Screaming Frog save dialog showing a CSV file with 'internal' in its filename located in the '~/Documents/SEO/exports/' (or '/home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/') directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the validation report file was created at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal, or file manager showing the file 'hreflang_report.txt' located in the '~/Documents/SEO/reports/' (or '/home/ga/Documents/SEO/reports/') directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the report contains a substantial summary of findings including page counts, errors, and recommendations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'hreflang_report.txt' visible in a text editor or terminal, showing a block of text (at least 300 characters) that discusses hreflang validation, mentions page counts, identifies errors, and provides recommendations.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the Screaming Frog GUI to perform the crawl and exports, rather than mocking the files via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Screaming Frog interface being actively used (e.g., clicking export buttons, viewing tabs) rather than just terminal commands creating CSV files with fake data.

integrity.description: Ensure the report content appears to be based on actual crawl data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in 'hreflang_report.txt' should contain specific details (like actual numbers for page counts or specific hreflang errors found on crawler-test.com) rather than generic placeholder text or verbatim repetition of the prompt.

integrity.description: Ensure the exported files are genuine CSV files.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the contents of the exported files are visible, they should display comma-separated values matching Screaming Frog export formats, not empty files or plain text masquerading as CSVs.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target URL to be crawled is exactly 'https://crawler-test.com/'. The exported CSV files must be saved in '/home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports' and their filenames must contain 'hreflang' and 'internal' respectively. The validation report must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/SEO/reports/hreflang_report.txt'. A valid report should be at least 300 characters long and contain keywords relevant to the audit such as 'hreflang', 'language', 'return', 'tag', 'missing', 'error', and 'recommendation'.
